_ai: AI fills this. Derived from: bounded-contexts.json + user-journeys.json + architecture.json. Do not edit manually.

events.name: tenant.provisioned

consumers.reason: send welcome email to tenant admin (UJ001 step 3)
activate Stripe subscription for the chosen plan (FR001)

events.trigger: Tenant admin submits registration form (UJ001 step 1). tenant-service writes Tenant row and OutboxRecord in the same transaction (ADR005, NFR003). Related: FR001, BR005.
events.idempotencyMechanism: notification-service and billing-service deduplicate via InboxRecord on event_id (NFR009).
events.channel: SNS topic: tenant-provisioned-topic → SQS FIFO queues: notification-service-tenant-provisioned-queue, billing-service-tenant-provisioned-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: correlation_id (UUID — propagated from API Gateway, NFR014)
stripe_customer_id (string — created by tenant-service before publishing)

events.name: tenant.configured

consumers.reason: refresh local TenantConfig cache to enforce updated booking rules (BR002, BR008, BR010)
invalidate slot availability cache for the tenant (NFR005)

events.trigger: Tenant admin updates business hours, cancellation policy, booking window, or max_advance_bookings (FR002, UJ001 steps 4–6). Related: BR002, BR003, BR008, BR010.
events.idempotencyMechanism: Consumers deduplicate via InboxRecord on event_id. Config snapshot in payload means out-of-order delivery is safe — consumers apply the latest snapshot by comparing occurred_at.
events.channel: SNS topic: tenant-configured-topic → SQS FIFO queues: booking-command-service-tenant-configured-queue, availability-service-tenant-configured-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: config_snapshot (object: cancellation_hours, booking_window_days, max_advance_bookings, timezone, currency)

events.name: tenant.suspended

consumers.reason: halt acceptance of new booking requests for the tenant
notify tenant admin of suspension (optional in v1)

events.trigger: Platform Operator initiates suspension via ops-service (FR013, UJ007 step 5). Related: BR011.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id.
events.channel: SNS topic: tenant-suspended-topic → SQS FIFO queues per consumer. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: data_export_deadline (ISO 8601 date — 30 days from occurred_at, BR011)

events.name: tenant.deprovisioned

consumers.reason: initiate 90-day data retention countdown and schedule hard deletion (BR012, FR013)

events.trigger: Platform Operator confirms deprovisioning after the 30-day suspension window (FR013, UJ007 step 5). Related: BR011, BR012.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id.
events.channel: SNS topic: tenant-deprovisioned-topic → SQS FIFO queue: ops-service-tenant-deprovisioned-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: hard_deletion_at (ISO 8601 date — 90 days from occurred_at, BR012)

events.name: tenant.data_exported

consumers.reason: send download link email to verified tenant owner (FR012)

events.trigger: Tenant admin requests data export (FR012, UJ007 step 1–3). Related: BR014.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id.
events.channel: SNS topic: tenant-data-exported-topic → SQS FIFO queue: notification-service-tenant-data-exported-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: owner_email (string — delivery address for download link)
signed_url (string — S3 pre-signed URL, expires in 24 hours, FR012)
export_includes (array of strings — data categories included in archive)

events.name: appointment.requested

consumers.reason: send optional booking-in-progress acknowledgement to customer (FR007)

events.trigger: Customer submits booking request (UJ002 step 4–5). booking-command-service writes AppointmentEvent (type=requested) and OutboxRecord in the same transaction after passing idempotency and business-rule checks. Related: FR004, BR006, BR008.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id. Booking-level idempotency enforced upstream via BookingIdempotencyKey (BR006) before this event is ever published.
events.channel: SNS topic: appointment-requested-topic → SQS FIFO queue: notification-service-appointment-requested-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.

events.name: appointment.confirmed

consumers.reason: send booking confirmation to customer and staff (FR007, BR007)
increment confirmed_count and net_revenue on TenantDashboardView (FR008, NFR008)
update per-tenant booking velocity window (FR009)
invalidate Redis slot cache for the confirmed slot (NFR005)

events.trigger: Booking saga completes all steps successfully: slot reserved + payment captured + AppointmentEvent(confirmed) written (UJ002 step 7, FR004). This is the critical-path event — confirmation must be published within 30 seconds of booking (FR004 acceptance criterion). Related: FR004, BR001, BR004, BR006.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id in all consumers. dashboard-service uses last_event_id watermark on TenantDashboardView for safe incremental updates (NFR009).
events.channel: SNS topic: appointment-confirmed-topic → SQS FIFO queues: notification-service-appointment-confirmed-queue, dashboard-service-appointment-confirmed-queue, analytics-service-appointment-confirmed-queue, availability-service-appointment-confirmed-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: correlation_id (UUID — for distributed trace linkage across the saga, NFR014)
service_snapshot (object: service_id, name, duration_minutes, price_amount, currency)
payment_id (UUID, nullable — null if no payment required)

events.name: appointment.cancelled

consumers.reason: initiate refund if a payment was captured (FR005, BR009)
send cancellation notice to customer and staff (FR007, BR007)
increment cancelled_count and recalculate cancellation_rate (FR008)
invalidate slot cache to re-surface freed slot for new bookings (FR005)
update per-tenant cancellation rate window (FR009)

events.trigger: Customer or staff requests cancellation within the policy window (UJ003 step 1–3). booking-command-service validates BR002 (policy window), writes AppointmentEvent(cancelled), releases SlotLock, and publishes via Outbox in one transaction. Related: FR005, BR002, BR009, BR013.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication in all consumers. payment-service checks Payment.status before attempting refund to avoid duplicate refunds.
events.channel: SNS topic: appointment-cancelled-topic → SQS FIFO queues: payment-service-appointment-cancelled-queue, notification-service-appointment-cancelled-queue, dashboard-service-appointment-cancelled-queue, availability-service-appointment-cancelled-queue, analytics-service-appointment-cancelled-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: amount_eligible_for_refund (decimal, nullable — null if no payment or refund not applicable)

events.name: appointment.rescheduled

consumers.reason: send rescheduling confirmation to customer (FR007, FR006)
increment rescheduled_count on TenantDashboardView (FR008)
invalidate cache for old slot (now freed) and new slot (now locked)

events.trigger: Customer selects a new slot for an existing confirmed appointment (UJ004, FR006). booking-command-service first confirms the new slot via saga, then releases the old SlotLock and writes AppointmentEvent(rescheduled) atomically. Related: FR006, BR001, BR004.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id in all consumers.
events.channel: SNS topic: appointment-rescheduled-topic → SQS FIFO queues: notification-service-appointment-rescheduled-queue, dashboard-service-appointment-rescheduled-queue, availability-service-appointment-rescheduled-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.

events.name: appointment.completed

consumers.reason: update peak_hour computation on TenantDashboardView (FR008)

events.trigger: Appointment slot time has passed and the appointment was not cancelled. In v1, a scheduled job in booking-command-service transitions confirmed appointments to completed after slot_end + 15 minutes. Related: FR008.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id.
events.channel: SNS topic: appointment-completed-topic → SQS FIFO queue: dashboard-service-appointment-completed-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.

events.name: appointment.failed

consumers.reason: send failure acknowledgement to customer (optional — not in MVP)

events.trigger: Booking saga fails: either the slot is unavailable (409) or payment capture fails (402) (FR004, BR001, BR004). booking-command-service writes compensation events and publishes appointment.failed via Outbox.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id.
events.channel: SNS topic: appointment-failed-topic → SQS FIFO queue: notification-service-appointment-failed-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: appointment_id (UUID, nullable — may not exist if saga failed before Appointment row was created)

events.name: payment.captured

consumers.reason: advance booking saga to appointment.confirmed step after payment confirmation (FR004, BR004)

events.trigger: Stripe PaymentIntent charge confirmed during booking saga step 2 (UJ002 step 6, FR004). payment-service writes Payment record and publishes via Outbox. Related: BR004.
events.idempotencyMechanism: booking-command-service checks BookingIdempotencyKey before re-advancing the saga (BR006). InboxRecord deduplication on event_id.
events.channel: SNS topic: payment-captured-topic → SQS FIFO queue: booking-command-service-payment-captured-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.

events.name: payment.failed

consumers.reason: trigger slot release compensation and publish appointment.failed (FR004, BR004)

events.trigger: Stripe PaymentIntent charge declines or times out during booking saga step 2 (UJ002, FR004). Related: BR004.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id. booking-command-service checks SlotLock.released_at before attempting compensation.
events.channel: SNS topic: payment-failed-topic → SQS FIFO queue: booking-command-service-payment-failed-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.

events.name: payment.refunded

consumers.reason: send refund confirmation to customer (optional enhancement in v1.0)

events.trigger: Stripe refund confirmed after appointment.cancelled event processed by payment-service (UJ003 step 4, FR005). Related: BR009.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id. payment-service checks Payment.status = captured before processing refund.
events.channel: SNS topic: payment-refunded-topic → SQS FIFO queue: notification-service-payment-refunded-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.

events.name: payment.refund_failed

consumers.reason: escalate to DLQ for manual resolution — refund failure must not block slot release (BR009, FR010)

events.trigger: Stripe refund API returns an error after appointment.cancelled (UJ003, BR009). Slot is already released and customer already notified. Only the refund step has failed. Related: BR009.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id in ops-service.
events.channel: SNS topic: payment-refund-failed-topic → SQS FIFO queue: payment-service-appointment-cancelled-queue-dlq (routed after 4 retries). MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: stripe_payment_intent_id (string — for manual refund via Stripe Dashboard)

events.name: notification.sent
events.trigger: notification-service successfully delivers a notification via SendGrid, Twilio, or Twilio WhatsApp (FR007). Updates NotificationRecord.status = sent.
events.idempotencyMechanism: Not consumed by any downstream service in v1. Published for future analytics and audit purposes.
events.channel: SNS topic: notification-sent-topic (no active subscribers in MVP). MessageGroupId = tenant_id.

events.name: notification.failed

consumers.reason: raise alert if DLQ depth grows — notification failure after 4 retries indicates a systemic delivery problem (FR007, NFR016)

events.trigger: notification-service exhausts 4 retry attempts for a notification delivery (FR007). NotificationRecord.status = dlq. Related: BR007, FR007.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id in ops-service.
events.channel: SNS topic: notification-failed-topic → SQS FIFO queue: ops-service-notification-failed-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: attempt_count (integer — always 4 when this event is published)
trigger_event_id (UUID — the AppointmentEvent that triggered this notification)

events.name: analytics.velocity_spike_detected

consumers.reason: deliver velocity spike alert to tenant admin (FR009)
log platform-level anomaly for operator visibility (FR009)

events.trigger: analytics-service detects booking velocity > 3x the tenant's historical 5-minute average (FR009). Calculated over a sliding 5-minute Kinesis window. Related: FR009. In scope from v1.0 per roadmap.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id in all consumers.
events.channel: SNS topic: analytics-velocity-spike-topic → SQS FIFO queues: notification-service-analytics-alert-queue, ops-service-analytics-alert-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.

events.name: analytics.cancellation_rate_alert

consumers.reason: deliver cancellation rate alert to tenant admin (FR009)
log platform-level anomaly (FR009)

events.trigger: analytics-service detects cancellation_rate > 30% over the last 50 bookings for a tenant (FR009). Related: FR009. In scope from v1.0 per roadmap.
events.idempotencyMechanism: InboxRecord deduplication on event_id in all consumers.
events.channel: SNS topic: analytics-cancellation-alert-topic → SQS FIFO queues: notification-service-analytics-alert-queue, ops-service-analytics-alert-queue. MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
keyPayloadFields: sample_size (integer — number of bookings in the window, always 50)

events.name: replay.started
events.trigger: Platform Operator initiates a tenant event replay via the ops console (FR011, UJ006 step 5). ops-service creates a ReplayJob record and publishes this event. Related: FR011.
events.idempotencyMechanism: No active downstream subscribers in v1 — consumed by monitoring/Grafana via log ingestion.
events.channel: SNS topic: replay-started-topic (no active SQS subscribers; consumed via log pipeline for Grafana visibility). MessageGroupId = tenant_id.

events.name: replay.completed
events.trigger: ops-service finishes re-publishing all scoped AppointmentEvents to SNS (FR011, UJ006 step 6). ReplayJob.status = completed.
events.idempotencyMechanism: No active downstream subscribers in v1 — consumed via log pipeline.
events.channel: SNS topic: replay-completed-topic (no active SQS subscribers). MessageGroupId = tenant_id.
